data.title: GMR Airports Infrastructure Unit to Stake in Megawide GMR Construction JV
data.description:
GMR Airports Infrastructure subsidiary, GMR Airports International, has agreed to divest its entire 50% stake in the Megawide GMR Construction JV to its joint venture partner, Megawide Construction for 80 million Philippine pesos, according to a Thursday filing with the Indian bourses.

Megawide GMR Construction was involved in the engineering, procurement, and construction works for Clark Airport, but has not carried out operations since completing the EPC works in 2020, GMR Airports said in the filing.

data.title: Ubisoft drops to 3-month lows after game delay, outlook cut
data.description:
** Shares in Ubisoft tumble as much as 10.8% to their lowest since early October after the video game maker postponed "Assassin's Creed Shadows" premiere again and cut its Q3 and FY net bookings outlook

** Morningstar analysts note the second debut delay points to "larger fundamental issues" at the firm, as it adjusts its "uncertainty rating" for Ubisoft to "very high"

** J.P.Morgan cuts its estimates for "Assassin's Creed Shadows" unit sales to 200 mln euros from 350 mln euros in Q4, trimming its PT on the stock by 9% to 10 euros

** The firm said also it had appointed advisors to "review and pursue various transformational... options", a move that TD Cowen analysts view with skepticism

** "... would-be buyers have had plenty of chances to speak up over the last few years," the analysts said in a note, adding that interested parties may wait for matters at the company to worsen further

** At 0839 GMT the stock was down 7.3%, at the bottom of France's SBF120 index

data.title: China Central Bank's Record Offshore Yuan Issuance Marks 'Firm' Effort to Anchor Weak Currency, Standard Chartered Says
data.description:
The Chinese central bank's record 60 billion yuan offshore issuance marks a clear move to curb this type of funding and prevent further depreciation in the currency, Standard Chartered said in a Thursday note.

The issuance, the largest in the central bank's history, comes after recent weakness in the onshore US dollar-yuan () spot rate, the equity research firm said.

The move, along with the recent tightening of the liquidity conditions for the offshore market-based CNH currency, points to a "firm stance" by The People's Bank of China to support the onshore yuan's (CNY) current position, analyst Becky Liu said.

Standard Chartered expects tight CNH liquidity conditions to continue in the near term, possibly even beyond the Lunar New Year, and the cap in the below 7.35 to remain in the "foreseeable future."

A more visible direction for US policies under Donald Trump's administration should mainly trigger Chinese authorities to reassess the foreign exchange policy, the research firm said.

data.title: Engie Consortium to Expand Egyptian Wind Farm Project to 650 MW
data.description:
Engie plans to expand its flagship Red Sea Wind Energy wind farm in Ras Ghareb, Egypt, to 650 megawatts from 500 megawatts.

The French energy company said Friday that 306 megawatts of capacity was connected to the national grid in the project's first phase ahead of scheduled and full commissioning of the farm is planned for the third quarter.

The project's entire production was purchased by the Egyptian Electricity Transmission Company under a 25-year power purchase agreement.

Engie and Orascom Construction own 35% and 25% stakes in the project, respectively, while Toyota Tsusho and Eurus Energy hold 20% each.

data.title: Sanhe Pile's Parent to Raise Stake in Company
data.description:
Guangdong Sanhe Pile's controlling shareholder, Guangdong Sanhe Building Materials Group, plans to raise its holdings in the company, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

Sanhe Building Materials' shares will be increased to 80 million yuan from 40 million yuan.

The stake increase will be made until July 9, the Chinese building materials manufacturer said.

Sanhe Pile's shares closed over 1% lower.

data.title: Career Point Gets Board Nod to Change Company's Name
data.description:
Educational services provider Career Point has received board approval to change of the company's name to CP Capital, according to a Friday filing to the stock exchanges.

The name will be changed on receipt of approval from Ministry of Corporate Affairs of India subject to name availability.

Shares of the company were down 1% in recent trade.

data.title: UK's Sainsbury's bottoms FTSE 100 on retaining annual profit outlook
data.description:
** Shares of Sainsbury's , Britain's second-biggest supermarket group down 2.2% at 257.4p; top loser on FTSE 100 index CURRENCYCOM:UK100

** Forecasts FY underlying operating profit in line with consensus and midpoint of forecast of 1.01 bln-1.06 bln pounds ($1.24 bln-$1.30 bln)

** Posts 2.8% rise in underlying sales for Christmas quarter while sales at its Argos, co's multi-category retailer, falls 1.4%

** General merchandise and clothing sales in SBRY's stores down 0.1% in Q3

** As of last close, stock down 3.8% YTD

($1 = 0.8139 pounds)

data.title: What to Watch in Today's Jobs Report — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

  • The U.S. economy likely added 155,000 jobs in December, according to a consensus forecast of economists polled by The Wall Street Journal.
  • That compares with 227,000 new positions in November.
  • The unemployment rate is seen holding steady at 4.2%.
  • The nonfarm payrolls report, issued by the Labor Department's Bureau of Labor Statistics, is due at 8:30 a.m. ET.
  • The data will be an important input for Federal Reserve policymakers ahead of their next interest-rate decision, due Jan. 29.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Shenzhen Consys Science & Technology Completes Trial Production of New Generation Processors
data.description:
Shenzhen Consys Science & Technology subsidiary Shenzhen Koss Technology completed trial production and tape-out for its new generation processors, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai Stock Exchange.

The company will continue with comprehensive testing of the products for wireless digital signal processing and protocol stack processing, the filing said.

data.title: UK's Alliance Pharma soars after agreeing to DBAY takeover
data.description:
** Shares of Alliance Pharma Plc surge as much as 39% to 61.5p, stock top pct gainer across London

** Stock set to post best day ever, if trend holds

** UK healthcare group agrees to be acquired by asset management firm DBAY Advisors in an all-cash deal valuing it at 349.7 mln pounds ($429.6 mln)

** The 62.5p-per-share offer represents a premium of about 41% to stock's Thursday close

** Acquisition is expected to be completed in H1 of 2025; Alliance board says it intends to unanimously recommend that shareholders vote in favour of the deal

** ALAPH gained 13% in 2024

data.title: Serco Meeting Guidance Seen as Challenging as Headwinds Remain — Market Talk
data.description: U.K. outsourcing company Serco's shares face headwinds from immigration and U.K. national insurance contracts, Jefferies analysts say in a note. Among other items, the loss of an Australian contract and an expected reduction in U.K. immigration will create a 20% profit headwind of around 55 million pounds over 2025-2026, Jefferies says. "Easing mobilization costs, continued efficiency/productivity gains, cost reductions in Australia and mix benefits from U.S. growth are offsets but meeting new 260 million pounds FY25 profit guidance may still be a challenge," Jefferies says. Jefferies downgrades its rating to hold from buy and lowers its target price to 175 pence from 225 pence. Shares are down 1.5% at 149.10 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0836 GMT - U.K. outsourcing company Serco's shares face headwinds from immigration and U.K. national insurance contracts, Jefferies analysts say in a note. Among other items, the loss of an Australian contract and an expected reduction in U.K. immigration will create a 20% profit headwind of around 55 million pounds over 2025-2026, Jefferies says. "Easing mobilization costs, continued efficiency/productivity gains, cost reductions in Australia and mix benefits from U.S. growth are offsets but meeting new 260 million pounds FY25 profit guidance may still be a challenge," Jefferies says. Jefferies downgrades its rating to hold from buy and lowers its target price to 175 pence from 225 pence. Shares are down 1.5% at 149.10 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0834 GMT - Singapore's economic growth will likely remain resilient and above potential this year, Nomura economists write in a note. Singapore's free-trade agreement with the U.S., plus strong local wage and employment growth, will likely help cushion against shocks from rising trade protectionism, they say. Nomura maintains its 2025 GDP growth forecast at 2.8%, near the top end of the official 1.0%-3.0% forecast range. This reflects the brokerage's view that electronics exports will stay supportive of manufacturing output growth in 1H. However, the forecast also represents a moderation from growth of 4.0% last year, partly due to expected weaker external demand under Trump 2.0, they say.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0820 GMT - Alibaba could post accelerated growth in gross merchandise volume for fiscal 3Q compared with fiscal 2Q as its market share has continued to stabilize, HSBC analysts say in a note. Both China's trade-in program and the November shopping festival supported the company's accelerated GMV growth, the analysts say. Competition from ByteDance's Douyin and Tencent's WeChat should remain manageable, the analysts say, as they think Alibaba will remain committed to investing in user experience to defend market share. "Continual monetization improvement in China e-commerce coupled with recovery in earnings growth thereafter is crucial for a share price re-rating," the analysts say. HSBC projects the e-commerce giant's fiscal 3Q revenue to grow 8% on year. HSBC maintains a buy call on Allibaba with its ADR target price unchanged at $128.00. The ADRs last closed at $83.69. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0815 GMT - Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index closed 0.9% lower at 19064.29, erasing earlier gains. Investors are waiting for U.S. nonfarm payrolls data due later, which could influence the Federal Reserve's rate cut trajectory. Expectations around the Fed's rate path affect appetite for riskier assets like stocks. Chinese property developer Sunac China slumped 26% after the company received a winding up petition. Other decliners include JD Health International, which dropped 5.1%, and Lenovo Group, which lost 4.9%. Meanwhile, Country Garden Services rose 5.2% and Zijin Mining Group added 3.1%. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0815 GMT - Sainsbury's 3Q update was positive, with sales growth against a challenging comparison point, Shore Capital analysts Clive Black and Darren Shirley write. The British grocer's unchanged expectations for retail adjusted operating profit are seen as reassuring, they say. Moreover, adjusted operating profit for its financial services division is now guided to be around 30 million pounds, leading to a 10 million pound upgrade to the U.K. investment group's pretax profit estimate. The Argos business held its own, with a 1.4% drop in sales amid a tough market but growth in the Christmas period, the analysts say. Sainsbury's looks particularly attractive given significant assets and cash generation, together with market share gains and a dividend yield of 5.2%, they say. Shares are down 2.0% at 25.78 pounds. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

0750 GMT - ComfortDelGro faces increased competition in 2025, analysts at UOB Kay Hian write in a note. The Singapore-based company raises its taxi platform fees to offset a new industry-wide rule requiring higher contributions to platform workers' state-run pension plans. Meanwhile, two new competitors enter the ride-hailing market, further intensifying competition, the analysts note. A bus contract currently operated by ComfortDelGro's subsidiary, SBS Transit, is up for tender. This poses a near-term earnings risk due to competitive bidding, which is likely to result in lower margins, they add. UOB Kay Hian reduces its 2025-2026 profit after tax and minority interests estimates by 2%-3%. While maintaining a buy rating, the brokerate trims its target price to S$1.77 from S$1.83. Shares are flat at S$1.44. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0747 GMT - Airbus's plane deliveries for 2024 came 4% short of the company's original guidance, but the 766 deliveries it made are essentially in line with consensus estimates and the company's own downgraded target, Berenberg analysts say. The European plane maker delivered 123 planes in December, which is 10%, or 11 planes, more compared with December 2023, the analysts say in a research note. The total for last month, while impressive, "is likely to lead to a very slow start to 2025 and the increasing Q4 weighting is becoming progressively harder to get comfortable with," the analysts say. Such a push in the last month of the year has historically been followed by light deliveries in the first quarter of the following year, the analysts say. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0741 GMT - Sky ICT is likely a main beneficiary of rising IT investment by Thailand's government, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Wasu Mattanapotchanart says in a research report. The professional solutions integrator will probably win IT projects this calendar year from five government agencies, including the Ministry of Interior, which have a combined IT spending budget totaling THB39 billion for fiscal 2025, 20% higher than the fiscal 2024 budget, the analyst says. The brokerage reiterates the stock's buy rating on expected 2025 core profit growth of 56% for the company, underpinned by growing airport passenger flows and government spending. It has a target price of THB30.10 on the shares, which are 0.85% lower at THB23.20. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0739 GMT - The FTSE 100 is expected to open 15 points lower, or 0.2%, according to IG. The index closed up 68 points, or 0.8%, to 8319 points on Thursday. A weaker sterling boosted the large proportion of overseas earners listed on the index Thursday. Sterling fell following a sharp rise in U.K. government bond yields amid concerns about rising U.K. borrowing and a weak U.K. economic outlook. Investors are now looking ahead to the U.S. nonfarm payrolls report at 1330 GMT for clues on the Federal Reserve's interest rate path. Stronger-than-expected data would push back expectations for another rate cut until June, while softer-than-forecast data would strengthen bets for a May cut, Swissquote Bank's Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a note. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0734 GMT - Nordic markets are seen opening slightly lower, with IG calling the OMXS30 down 0.1% at around 2547. Markets were quiet Thursday with the U.S. partially closed in honor of ex-president Jimmy Carter, SEB analysts say in a note. European stock markets generally closed in positive territory, while Asian stock markets have ticked lower this morning ahead of Friday's U.S. jobs data. Futures indicate a tentative opening in both the U.S. and Europe. "The direction of the U.S. labor market is crucial for the Federal Reserve, and any unexpected weakness (or strength) could trigger significant market reactions," SEB says. OMXS30 closed at 2549.58, OMXN40 at 2536.42 and OBX at 1379.37. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0734 GMT - Chinese shares fell, with all sectors closing in the red, led by losses in consumer-related stocks. Investor sentiment remained weak after China's consumer prices posted a modest 0.2% growth in 2024, reflecting ongoing deflationary pressures. Midea Group fell 3.0% and Gree Electric Appliances dropped 2.3%. In the consumer services sector, China Tourism Group Duty Free Corp. lost 1.7%, while Shanghai Jinjiang International Hotels declined 3.1%. Among the few gainers, Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corp. rose 2.0% and SAIC Motor Corp. added 1.3%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index ended 1.3% lower at 3168.52, the Shenzhen Composite Index fell 2.2% and the ChiNext Price Index declined 1.8%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0721 GMT - BBVA's reduction of the acceptance level of its $12 billion-plus hostile bid to take over Banco de Sabadell is mostly a technical adjustment at the request of Spanish regulators, RBC Capital Markets' Benjamin Toms and Pablo de la Torre Cuevas say in a research note. BBVA said its offer requires more than half of the effective voting rights of Sabadell, excluding treasury shares. Regulators saw a need to clarify what BBVA's acceptance threshold was and the Spanish bank's move effectively means the minimum acceptance is reduced by 0.7%, according to RBC. "We continue to be of the view that this deal gets done, probably by [the first half], and probably with some relatively minor remedies and a 10% increase in the offer price," the analysts say. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: Baazar Style Retail Opens Two New Stores in India
data.description:
Baazar Style Retail opened two new stores in India on Friday, taking its total store count to 201, according to a same-day disclosure to the stock exchanges.

The company opened two new Style Baazar stores, one at Cuttack in Odisha, India, and another at Mau in Uttar Pradesh.

Shares of the company were down nearly 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Cosco Capital Repurchases More Shares
data.description:
Cosco Capital bought back 300,000 shares at 5.5 Philippine pesos apiece, according to a Friday Philippine bourse disclosure.

The retail holding company has so far spent 2.03 billion pesos out of the 4 billion pesos allocated under its buyback program.

data.title: UK's Haleon falls after MS downgrade to 'equal-weight'
data.description:
** Shares of Haleon fall 1.6% to 376.9p - among the top losers on FTSE 100 index CURRENCYCOM:UK100

** Morgan Stanley downgrades stock to 'equal-weight' from 'overweight'; raises PT to 410p from 400p

** Brokerage says after a year of significant outperformance, stock has less room for further substantial outperformance in near term

** "On the downside, weaker consumer demand, an inability to improve margins in 2025, and/or more divestments could all lead to earnings downgrades and/or de-rating" - MS

** Brokerage says it prefers Reckitt in consumer health segment

** HLN rose ~16% in 2024

data.title: Pulike Biological Engineering Director to Trim Stake
data.description:
Pulike Biological Engineering said a director plans to trim his stake in the company from Feb. 11 to May 11.

Hu Wei currently holds 4,186,720 company shares, equivalent to a 1.2% stake, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of Pulike Biological Engineering closed 4% lower Friday.

data.title: Action Might Be Needed to Steady U.K. Gilt Market — Market Talk
data.description: Yields on U.K. government bonds, or gilts, haven't reached unsustainable levels but action needs to be taken to steady markets, potentially by the Bank of England, Mediolanum International Funds Limited's Daniel Loughney says. Gilt yields have surged this week, with the 30-year yield hitting its highest since 1998 Thursday. "I don't think we are yet at the point of no return but if actions are not taken to calm the markets' fiscal concerns it is hard to see what will stem the tide from a domestic perspective," Mediolanum's head of fixed income says. "Fiscal sustainability will be at risk if yields continue to rise." Still, the gilt-yield rise "is not happening independently of trends in other government bond markets." (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Atlas Copco Buys UK's Medi-teknique
data.description:
Atlas Copco (ATCO-A.ST, ATCO-B.ST) acquired British medical gas maintenance and service company Medi-teknique, according to a Friday release.

The Swedish industrial group said Medi-teknique will become part of its medical gas solutions division in the compressor technique business area. The deal is expected to broaden Atlas Copco's regional reach and presence in the aftermarket and installation sector.

The purchase price was not disclosed.

data.title: Austria Industrial Output Falls to 5-Month Low
data.description:
Industrial production in Austria fell by 2.7% year-on-year in November 2024, deepening from an upwardly revised 2.5% decline in October.

This marked the eleventh consecutive month of contraction and the steepest drop since June, driven primarily by a sharp fall in energy goods (-11% vs. 0.2% in October).

Additionally, output declined further for consumer durables (-14.6% vs. -12%) and continued to fall, albeit at a slower pace, for intermediate goods (-3.8% vs. -6.9%) and capital goods (-5.3% vs. -5.8%).

In contrast, production of consumer non-durables rebounded by 1.1% after a 0.1% decline in October.

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial activity decreased by 0.6%, following a 0.1% rise in the prior period.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: UBS Group Nears Deal to Settle Credit Suisse US Tax Scofflaw Case
data.description:
(Updates with response from the Department of Justice in the final paragraph.)

UBS Group is close to agreeing to pay hundreds of millions of dollars to settle alleged violations of US tax laws by defunct Credit Suisse, The Wall Street Journal reported Thursday, citing unnamed people familiar with the matter.

A settlement with the US Justice Department could come as early as this week, the report said.

Credit Suisse, which was taken over by UBS following its 2023 collapse, agreed to pay $2.6 billion and shut down accounts of alleged American tax cheats but allegedly failed to provide US officials with the whereabouts of moved funds, leaving it open to further penalties, according to the news outlet.

The Department of Justice declined to comment while UBS did not reply to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Windlas Biotech Gets Good Manufacturing Practices Certification for New Injectable Unit
data.description:
Pharmaceutical formulations contract development and manufacturing company Windlas Biotech has received good manufacturing practices certification from the Food Safety & Drugs Administration Authority of Uttarakhand, India for its new injectable facility.

The certification follows the inspection undertaken by the regulator at the new unit in December 2024.

Shares of the company were down 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Singapore's Economy Can Stay Resilient in Face of Rising Trade Protectionism — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's economic growth will likely remain resilient and above potential this year, Nomura economists write in a note. Singapore's free-trade agreement with the U.S., plus strong local wage and employment growth, will likely help cushion against shocks from rising trade protectionism, they say. Nomura maintains its 2025 GDP growth forecast at 2.8%, near the top end of the official 1.0%-3.0% forecast range. This reflects the brokerage's view that electronics exports will stay supportive of manufacturing output growth in 1H. However, the forecast also represents a moderation from growth of 4.0% last year, partly due to expected weaker external demand under Trump 2.0, they say.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0834 GMT - Singapore's economic growth will likely remain resilient and above potential this year, Nomura economists write in a note. Singapore's free-trade agreement with the U.S., plus strong local wage and employment growth, will likely help cushion against shocks from rising trade protectionism, they say. Nomura maintains its 2025 GDP growth forecast at 2.8%, near the top end of the official 1.0%-3.0% forecast range. This reflects the brokerage's view that electronics exports will stay supportive of manufacturing output growth in 1H. However, the forecast also represents a moderation from growth of 4.0% last year, partly due to expected weaker external demand under Trump 2.0, they say.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0821 GMT - Sterling trims losses sustained Thursday following a sharp rise in U.K. government bond yields as a U.K. Treasury spokesperson said meeting the fiscal rules is non-negotiable. That means Treasury chief Rachel Reeves could deliver some fiscal consolidation if the updated Office for Budget Responsibility forecasts in March show the government isn't on track to meet fiscal rules, ING's Francesco Pesole says in a note. "The market seems to be acknowledging the Treasury's reiterated fiscal pledges and this has prevented the gilt and pound selloffs from becoming disorderly." GBP/USD falls 0.2% to 1.2288 after hitting a 14-month low of 1.2239 Thursday, according to FactSet. EUR/GBP rises 0.1% to 0.8280 after reaching a two-month high of 0.8407 Thursday.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0813 GMT - The continued cooling of Singapore's underlying inflation supports Nomura's view that the central bank could ease monetary policy settings later this month. The brokerage's FX strategy team. now assigns a 55% likelihood to the MAS easing its policy via a slight reduction in the slope of S$NEER appreciation, up from 40% previously. The MAS likely views current policy settings as restrictive given the risk posed by Trump's tariffs, say Euben Paracuelles and Charnon Boonnuch in a note. Nomura continues to expect 2025 average core inflation at 1.6% on year, near the lower end of MAS's forecast range of 1.5%-3.0%. However, it sees downside risks after a broad-based, sharper-than-expected decline in November from October. MAS is due to announce its policy decision no later than Jan. 31. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0809 GMT - Bank Indonesia's strong emphasis on foreign-exchange stability will likely make it difficult to deliver additional rate cuts in the coming months, Nomura analysts say. That's particularly true given that President-elect Trump is threatening to raise tariffs on imports and the Fed is turning less dovish, Euben Paracuelles and Nabila Amani say in a note. The economists pencil in one 25bp cut in 4Q 2025. The Indonesian central bank meets next week. They see Indonesia's GDP growth slowing slightly from an estimated 5.0% expansion in 2024. Household spending will likely be held back by still-weak consumer sentiment in 1H before edging up in 2H as public-sector spending improves. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0758 GMT - The dollar rises ahead of U.S. nonfarm payrolls data that could provide hints on the timing of the Federal Reserve's next interest-rate cut. The balance of risks are tilted towards dollar gains as possibly robust jobs figures could "prompt markets to price out a March rate cut and potentially push the first fully-priced move beyond June," ING's Francesco Pesole says in a note. In the event of weaker-than-expected data, investors could trim dollar long positions that bet on its rise. However, these longs could be rebuilt at better levels ahead of key upcoming data and Donald Trump's January 20 presidential inauguration. The jobs data are due at 1330 GMT. The DXY dollar index rises 0.1% to 109.272.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0755 GMT - U.K. government bonds, or gilts, have fared worse than others within a recent global government bond selloff, but they could recover soon, Capital Economics's Hubert de Barochez says in a note. "We think that bonds will recover before long, with yields in the U.K. falling particularly sharply by the end of this year," the senior markets economist says in a note. Gilts started to perform weakly in mid-September and the selloff has been particularly acute at the long end of the curve, especially during a second phase since early December, he says. "To some extent, this has reflected a broader trend." Government bonds have sold off across the developed world, led by the U.S, on growing concerns about Trump's policies. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0747 GMT - The Monetary Authority of Singapore is in no rush to ease monetary policy settings, CIMB analysts say, reiterating their call for a hold until April. Although inflation appears to be easing, the central bank will likely err on the side of caution, they write in a note. MAS may want to wait to assess the impact of U.S. President-elect Trump's policies first, they say. Significant trade, growth and inflation uncertainty stemming from Trump's policies could lead to some market volatility after his inauguration later this month, they say. Other events to look out for include Singapore's budget in February and geopolitical risks in Eastern Europe, the analysts add.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0723 GMT - German Bund yields are likely to find support soon, according to Citi Research's starting point for 2025, rates strategist Jamie Searle says in a note. "The sell-off into [2024] year-end likely reflects a reassessment of how far the European Central Bank can cut if the Fed is anticipating just two 25bp cuts in 2025." This was combined with the market making room for heavy, front-loaded government bond issuance in the eurozone in January. "Given the space already made for supply via higher outright yields, we suspect the bulk of the sell-off may be over," Searle says. However, the global picture needs to be considered, "starting with the imminent payrolls release for December." The 10-year Bund yield is up 2.5 basis points at 2.555%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0705 GMT - The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield is gradually approaching the 5% threshold, and a further selloff is a buying opportunity, Societe Generale Research's rates strategists say in a note. "A momentum led sell-off in the 10-year Treasury toward 5% is a buying opportunity," they say. The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield is rising along with term premia and terminal fed funds rate expectations, they say. Term premia is the additional yield investors demand to hold a long-dated bond rather than a short-dated one. Declining equity risk premiums and a tightening of financial conditions are likely catalysts for a reversal, they say. The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield rises about 2 basis points to 4.698%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0701 GMT - The Bank of Japan's branch managers meeting this week supports Nomura analysts' forecast for a rate hike in March rather than January. The meeting highlights that more companies are recognizing the need for continued pay increases, which suggests potential wage growth in 2025. However, this doesn't necessarily confirm that wage hikes are becoming more broad-based, Nomura analysts Kyohei Morita and others write in a note. Looking ahead, the analysts expect the economy to be driven by private consumption supported by wage increases and capex focused on software investment, making economic activity more "home-grown." Still, uncertainties remain, including risks from political developments, domestic labor shortages and geopolitical tensions abroad. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0657 GMT - The Malaysian ringgit could remain under pressure with the coming U.S. December's non-farm payrolls report posing a key risk, Kenanga economists say in a note. A stronger-than-expected data print could weaken the ringgit further, while the coming U.S. CPI, PPI, and retail sales data may heighten inflation concerns and reinforce the Fed's less dovish stance, they reckon. Investors are expected to remain cautious ahead of Trump's inauguration, which could sustain dollar strength. External factors such as potential Bank of Japan interventions and China's growth outlook also warrant attention, the economists add. Kenanga pegs USD/MYR's short-term resistance at 4.508 and support at 4.484. It projects the pair at 4.571 in 1Q and 4.530 in 2Q next year. USD/MYR is flat at 4.5020. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0642 GMT - The Bank of Thailand is in no rush to cut again, Nomura economists say, expecting it to hold rates steady until April. "The BOT has emphasized the need to preserve policy space amid heightened uncertainty," Charnon Boonnuch and Euben Paracuelles write in a note. They expect it will keep the policy rate steady at 2.25% in February, before delivering 25bp cuts in April and August, when more evidence of economic weakness will have emerged. Softer global growth, trade protectionism and slow tourism recovery pose downside risks to the Thai economy, they add. Domestically, renewed political uncertainty could result in weak budget execution. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: European shares dip as bond yields rise ahead of US jobs data
data.description:
European shares edged lower on Friday, as elevated government bond yields weighed on stocks, with attention now turning to U.S. jobs data that may provide clues on the Federal Reserve's interest rate trajectory.

The pan-European STOXX 600 was down 0.1% by 0816 GMT, although set for its best weekly performance in six weeks.

Yield on European government bonds remained elevated, with the yield on the German 10-year bund (DE10YT=RR) hitting its highest level in six months. Utilities (.SX6P), often traded as a bond proxy, were down 1%.

The U.S. jobs report, set for release at 1330 GMT, is expected to reveal a moderation in job growth for December, though it is still anticipated to remain robust. The unemployment rate is likely to stay at 4.2%, supporting the Federal Reserve's cautious stance on interest rate cuts this year.

Early in the trade, the food and beverages sub-index (.SX3P) was among the top losers in the STOXX, with alcohol manufacturers such as Pernod Ricard and Heineken declining about 1% each.

Among individual stocks, Ubisoft fell 8% after the French gaming giant announced another delay in the release of its blockbuster franchise Assassin's Creed.

data.title: Ubisoft Shares Fall After Slump in Net Bookings, Assassin's Creed Launch Delay
data.description:
By Maitane Sardon

Shares in Ubisoft Entertainment dropped after the French videogame maker said it expects a decrease in third-quarter net bookings and pushed back the release of its blockbuster game Assassin's Creed Shadows.

Shares were 8% lower at 11.35 euros in European morning trade Friday. They are down 50% over the past 12 months.

Ubisoft said it expects around 300 million euros ($309 million) in net bookings for the three months to the end of December, down 52% on year and below its previous guidance of 380 million euros. It attributed the decline to lower-than-expected holiday sales and game setbacks.

Analysts polled by Visible Alpha had forecast net bookings of 385.5 million euros.

The company delayed the launch of Assassin's Creed Shadows a second time. The game, which was due out Feb. 14, will be released March. 20 instead, Ubisoft said.

Write to Maitane Sardon at maitane.sardon@wsj.com

data.title: HOB Biotech Gets China Nod for Two Detection Kits
data.description:
China's medical products administrator approved HOB Biotech subsidiary Jiangsu HOB Biotech's two detection kits, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai Stock Exchange.

The autoimmune myositis antibody spectrum 1 detection kit helps diagnose systemic sclerosis, polymyositis, dermatomyositis, and other connective tissue illnesses, the filing said.

The anti-phospholipase A2 receptor antibody IgG detection kit is used in human serum samples, the filling said.

data.title: Heimstaden AB (publ) Divests Danish Development Projects to Fredensborg 32 AS
data.description:
Heimstaden

Heimstaden AB (publ) ("Heimstaden") has today agreed to divest all of its shares in Thors Have MidCo ApS, Næsbyvej MidCo ApS and Atriet ApS (the "Target Companies") to Fredensborg 32 AS ("Fredensborg") for a total gross purchase price of DKK 849 million and cash at closing of approximately 341 million on a cash and debt free basis (corresponding to approximately SEK 1,308 million and SEK 525 million*, respectively). The transaction is expected to close in February 2025.

The Target Companies are, directly or indirectly, the holders of newly finalized or ongoing residential real estate projects in the Copenhagen region and in Odense, respectively, in total encompassing 591 units. The Target Companies had a book value of approximately DKK 790 million as per 31 December 2024 and the fair gross market value of the Target Companies is estimated to DKK 837.5 – 855.0 million (corresponding to approximately SEK 1,290 – 1,317 million) according to the fairness opinion obtained (see below).

The purchase price, on a cash and debt free basis, paid upon closing of the transaction amounts to approximately DKK 341 million (approximately SEK 525 million). The intention of Fredensborg is to divest the Target Companies (or properties included therein) within six years. When all Target Companies (or the underlying properties) have been divested within such time, Heimstaden has a right to receive an earn-out consideration. The earn-out consideration is time-limited and conditional upon Fredensborg having achieved an agreed internal rate of return (IRR) of 12 per cent per annum. The earn-out consideration would amount to 30 per cent of any excess return above the 12 per cent IRR.

The transaction is conditional upon the successful issue of new senior unsecured notes and completion of the tender offer announced by Heimstaden on 10 January 2025 in relation to its SEK 500 million floating rate notes due April 2025, its SEK 1,200 million floating rate notes due October 2025 and its EUR 350 million fixed rate notes due March 2026, as well as the approval by the current external finance providers of the Target Companies and/or their subsidiaries, respectively. Subject to the satisfaction of all closing conditions, the transaction is expected to close in February 2025.

The divestment follows the communicated strategy to continue the deleveraging of Heimstaden and the proceeds will be used for liability management.

Christian Fladeland, Chief Investment Officer and Co-CEO, comments:

- "The sale of our equity stakes in these entities allows for accelerating our deleveraging, whilst maintaining a possibility to capture future upside as the projects stabilise through strategic transaction structuring."

Related-Party Transaction

Fredensborg is a wholly owned subsidiary of Fredensborg 1994 AS, the indirect main shareholder of Heimstaden, and is controlled by the Chairperson of Heimstaden, Ivar Tollefsen, and is thus considered a related party within the meaning of the Swedish Annual Accounts Act.

As listed on Nasdaq First North Growth Market, Heimstaden is required to comply with good practice on the stock market, meaning amongst other things that certain related party transactions must be approved by the general meeting of Heimstaden. However, in its statement AMN 2023:45 concerning the particular circumstances in the company in terms of the character of the listed shares, the Swedish Securities Council (Sw. Aktiemarknadsnämnden) has stated that it is not required to apply the formal decision-making process set out in AMN 2019:25, provided that the shareholders are informed of the transaction by the company publishing information about the transaction corresponding to the information required by Chapter 16 a, Section 7, second paragraph of the Swedish Companies Act, as well as a fairness opinion regarding the fair market value of the transferred assets.

In view of the above, and as a basis for its decision to approve of the transaction, the board of directors has obtained a fairness opinion from CBRE A/S, as an independent valuation expert, regarding the fair market value of the Target Companies, which finds the purchase price from a financial point of view, within the range of what CBRE consider fair market value and has prepared a statement corresponding to Chapter 16 a, Section 7 of the Swedish Companies Act. The board of directors’ statement and the fairness opinion are both available on Heimstaden’s website and are attached to this press release.

Ivar Tollefsen has not taken part in the board of directors’ preparation of or in the board of directors’ resolution concerning the transaction.

*Based on the closing DKK/SEK exchange rate on the 9 January 2025. To be updated in accordance with the share purchase agreement concerning the transaction.

Contacts:

Frederik Berling, Media

+45 2130 9489, media@heimstaden.com

Cody Nelson, Investor Relations

+47 948 94 196, ir@heimstaden.com

About Heimstaden:

Heimstaden is a leading European residential real estate manager and investor with around 162,000 homes across nine countries with a property value of SEK 332 billion. We acquire, develop, and manage properties with an evergreen perspective. Guided by our Scandinavian heritage and values Dare, Care, and Share – we fulfil our mission to enrich and simplify our customers’ lives through Friendly Homes. Heimstaden is listed on Nasdaq First North Growth Market. Read more at www.heimstaden.com. Certified Adviser is Carnegie Investment Bank AB (publ).

This information is such information that Heimstaden AB (publ) is obliged to publish in accordance with the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted, through the agency of the above contact persons, for publication on 10 January 2025 at 09:35 CET.

Heimstaden AB Board Statement 16a

CBRE - Fairness Opinion 10Jan2025 signed

Christian Fladeland CO-CEO

data.title: E.ON shares fall after Romania puts planned asset sale under review
data.description:
Shares in E.ON fell on Friday after Romania's energy minister told the Financial Times that a planned disposal of a local division to Hungary's MVM was under review, partly due to fears of potential Russian influence.

Sebastian Burduja was quoted by the FT as saying that the planned sale of E.ON's 68% stake in E.ON Energie Romania could be blocked on security grounds.

E.ON had no immediate comment.

data.title: Counterfeit Banknotes in South Korea Drops to All-Time Low in 2024
data.description:
The number of counterfeit South Korean banknotes hit an all-time low of 143 in 2024, marking the lowest figure since data tracking began in 1998, Bank of Korea data indicated on Thursday.

The decline is largely attributed to the increasing use of noncash transactions such as online payments and credit cards. Among the fake bills, 5,000 won notes accounted for the majority, followed by 10,000 won and 50,000 won notes.
